subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Rate & Accumulation (Riemann Sums)
stimulus:
A customer at a gas station is pumping gasoline into a gas tank. The rate of flow of gasoline is modeled by a
differentiable function f , where f

parts.prompt:
Using correct units, interpret the meaning of ∫ f (t) dt in the context of the problem. Use a right
Riemann sum with the three subintervals [60, 90], [90, 120], and [120, 135] to approximate the value of
∫ f (t) dt.
rubric_criteria: pumped into the gas tank from time t = 60 seconds to time

parts.prompt:
Must there exist a value of c, for 60 < c < 120, such that f ¢(c) = 0 ? Justify your answer.
⎛ 2⎞
rubric_criteria: Must there exist a value of c, for 60 < c <120, such that f′(c) = 0? Justify your answer.
f is differentiable. ⇒ f is continuous on [60,120]. f(120) − f(60) = 0 1 point
parts.scoring_notes: Must there exist a value of c, for 60 < c <120, such that f′(c) = 0? Justify your answer.

parts.prompt:
The rate of flow of gasoline, in gallons per second, can also be modeled by g(t) = ⎛ ⎜ ⎜ ⎝ 50 t 0 ⎟ ⎟⎠ ⎟ ⎞ cos ⎜ ⎜ ⎜ ⎜⎝ ⎛ ⎜ ⎜ ⎝ 12 t 0 ⎟ ⎠⎟ ⎞ ⎟ ⎟ ⎟ ⎟ ⎟⎠ ⎟ for
0 £ t £ 150. Using this model, find the average rate of flow of gasoline over the time interval 0 £ t £ 150.
Show the setup for your calculations.

following response earns both points: ∫ g(t) dt =14.399504 so the average rate is 0.0959967.
